title: First-session onboarding and wayfinding proof
summary: Harden the first ten minutes so new players find the notebook, map, station, and first purpose without a tutorial panel.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane-1: Audit title/menu/map/station focus defaults and fix only the smallest first-session state or focus seams that block travel comprehension.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: A fresh player can reach the first purposeful outing and understand how to return to station using only the in-game shell.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Audit title/menu/map/station focus defaults and fix only the smallest first-session state or focus seams that block travel comprehension.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_334_target: Use guided nextBiomeId as the world-map focus fallback after route-marker support so first-session M + Enter points at Forest Trail after Shore Shelter and Coastal Scrub after Trail Stride.
systems_targets: keep route-marker support as the first preferred map focus when active
fall back to GuidedFieldSeasonState.nextBiomeId when present before falling back to the current map location
prove beach-beat-complete starter state opens the world map focused on forest with no menu cycling
prove station close after Trail Stride returns to the world map focused on coastal-scrub
preserve station-return and season-close-return map-menu default to field-station
non_goals: route-marker support behavior changes beyond preserving its existing priority

notes: route-marker support remains the first preferred world-map focus
guided nextBiomeId now becomes the fallback focus before current location
first-session runtime smoke proves Shore Shelter completion opens the map focused on forest
first-session runtime smoke proves station close after Trail Stride focuses coastal-scrub
station-return and season-close-return map-menu defaults still select field-station
verified: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first field-season guidance" passed
web-game client boot smoke passed with screenshot output/lane-1-main-334-browser/shot-0.png
custom browser proof for completed Shore Shelter save opened world-map focusedLocationId forest with no console/page errors
npm run build attempted but blocked by pre-existing unused declarations in src/test/field-requests.test.ts

notes: route-marker support remains the first preferred world-map focus
guided nextBiomeId fallback stays inside existing map/station/menu seams
first-session focused tests cover forest focus after Shore Shelter and coastal-scrub focus after Trail Stride
no tutorial panel, copy expansion, station redesign, route rewrite, save schema change, telemetry, or geometry change landed
verified: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first field-season guidance|route marker is already selected" passed
npm run build attempted but blocked outside lane 1 by a concurrent src/test/field-requests.test.ts type issue

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Cut and clarify onboarding copy around J/M/Enter, field station, first route, and first filed note while preserving kid-readable tone.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_335_target: Make the first-session title, menu, guided-season notice, Shore Shelter route copy, and first ready-to-file note read as one kid-readable loop: J for notebook, M for map/station, and Enter to choose or file.
recommended_files: src/engine/field-season-board.ts first Shore Shelter beat detail if needed
copy_targets: title hint names M directly before map/station
menu intro/helper pairs world-map travel with field-station filing/support
fresh-save NOTEBOOK TASK uses physical clue language before log/file language
Shore Shelter summary avoids token-like hyphenated clue wording where player-facing
first ready-to-file line points through M -> Field station -> Enter
non_goals: science fact rewrites beyond the first-route instruction copy
verification: npx vitest run src/test/overlay-copy.test.ts src/test/guided-field-season.test.ts src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first-session|guided field season|Shore Shelter|first field season"
npm run build if TypeScript runtime files change

verified: focused Vitest copy and first-route slices passed
npm run validate:agents passed with only the existing queue-size warning

notes: first-session title, menu, starter notice, Shore Shelter, and ready-to-file copy now read as a single J / M / Enter loop
route evidence ids, progression behavior, station state, save schema, world-map behavior, tutorial surfaces, geometry, and science facts stayed unchanged
review rechecked focused copy/first-route Vitest slices, npm run build, and git diff --check
first-session objective proof is tied to the existing beach-opening-shoulder frame
fresh screenshot/state/error targets stay under ignored output/alpha-screenshot-proof
pass conditions cover visible or nearby beach grass, active beach-shore-shelter, no large overlay, and no competing nearby travel target
geometry, route state, station/menu behavior, save schema, tutorial UI, support behavior, science copy, and committed screenshot output stayed unchanged

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Tune initial beach/coastal visual landmarks only if the screenshot proof shows the player lacks a physical first objective cue.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_336_target: Harden the first-session beach objective proof without changing geometry unless fresh screenshot/state evidence shows the player lacks a physical first cue.
pass_conditions: beach grass or authored dune-shoulder grass is visible or nearby at the first beach objective
`beach-shore-shelter` is active in the paired state
no large overlay covers the physical cue
no nearby travel target competes with the first objective
non_goals: new coastline branch or route objective
verification: if runtime or biome tests change, run focused beach/start tests plus `npm run build`

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Smoke first request activation and filing preview so the route loop teaches itself before support choices become relevant.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_337_target: Tighten the existing Shore Shelter runtime smoke so render_game_to_text proves first request activation, clue progress, ready-to-file state, station filing preview, filed notice, and next-route handoff without changing route behavior.
debug_checkpoints: fresh start exposes active beach-shore-shelter with title Shore Shelter, progress 0/3 stages, routeV2 gathering, and no evidence slots
after beach grass and driftwood inspect, serialized activeFieldRequest.routeV2 evidence slots advance through dune-grass and lee-cover
after bull kelp wrack inspect, serialized activeFieldRequest.routeV2 status is ready-to-synthesize, active progress is Ready To File, and fieldRequestNotice.variant is notebook-ready
field-station routeBoard.notebookReady previews beach-shore-shelter with SHORE SHELTER and the clue-backed filed sentence before Enter files it
filing clears routeV2Progress, records beach-shore-shelter, emits a filed-route notice, and hands activeFieldRequest to forest-hidden-hollow
coordination_notes: Lane 1 owns menu focus defaults and station/menu travel behavior.
Lane 2 owns exact onboarding wording around J, M, Enter, Shore Shelter, and ready-to-file copy.
Lane 3 owns first-session physical cue proof and beach geometry only if screenshot/state evidence contradicts the current start.
Prefer structural assertions over broad exact-copy assertions while lane 2 copy work is active.
verification: `npm test -- src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Shore Shelter"` or a narrower equivalent focused on the edited first route-loop assertions passes
`npm run validate:agents` passes after queue and packet updates

notes: Shore Shelter first-route runtime smoke now proves activation, routeV2 evidence progress, ready-to-file state, station preview, filing, and next-route handoff through existing debug output.

Route behavior, route definitions, station layout, menu focus, save schema, support choice, beach geometry, science content, onboarding copy, UI, telemetry, screenshots, and packet 133 matrix work stayed unchanged.
Shore Shelter first-route smoke now has deterministic route-loop proof through existing debug output.
The review found no behavior, UI, geometry, station, save, science, copy, telemetry, screenshot, or packet 133 matrix drift.
Focused Shore Shelter runtime smoke was rechecked and passed.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
